qa_pairs.question: Does the Alliance install Materials Studio centrally on all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Alliance does not have permission to install Materials Studio centrally on all clusters. Users with a license must install it in their own account.

qa_pairs.question: What command should I use if I am running the default 2020 standard software environment before installing Materials Studio?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are using the default 2020 standard software environment, you will need to use a command like `module load StdEnv/2016.4` before beginning, as the current instructions are only valid for older standard software environments.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to install Materials Studio 2020?
qa_pairs.answer: To install Materials Studio 2020, you need the archive file named `BIOVIA_2020.MaterialsStudio2020.tar` and the IP address (or DNS name) and port of an already configured licence server.

qa_pairs.question: Where should I upload the installer archive file for Materials Studio 2020?
qa_pairs.answer: You should upload the `BIOVIA_2020.MaterialsStudio2020.tar` file to your `/home` folder on the cluster you intend to use.

qa_pairs.question: What commands should I run after uploading the Materials Studio 2020 archive file?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run `export MS_LICENSE_SERVER=<port>@<server>` and `eb MaterialsStudio-2020.eb --sourcepath=$HOME`.

qa_pairs.question: What steps follow the completion of the Materials Studio 2020 installation commands?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the commands have completed, you should log out of the cluster and log back in. Then you can load the module.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load the Materials Studio 2020 module?
qa_pairs.answer: You can load the Materials Studio 2020 module with the command `module load materialsstudio/2020`.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done to allow Materials Studio 2020 to access the licence server from compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: You will need to contact technical support so that they can configure the firewall(s) to allow the software to connect to your licence server.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to install Materials Studio 2018?
qa_pairs.answer: To install Materials Studio 2018, you need the archive file named `MaterialsStudio2018.tgz` and the IP address (or DNS name) and port of an already configured licence server.

qa_pairs.question: Where should I upload the installer archive file for Materials Studio 2018?
qa_pairs.answer: You should upload the `MaterialsStudio2018.tgz` file to your `/home` folder on the cluster you intend to use.

qa_pairs.question: What commands should I run after uploading the Materials Studio 2018 archive file?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run `export MS_LICENSE_SERVER=<port>@<server>` and `eb /cvmfs/soft.computecanada.ca/easybuild/easyconfigs/m/MaterialsStudio/MaterialsStudio-2018.eb --disable-enforce-checksums --sourcepath=$HOME`.

qa_pairs.question: What steps follow the completion of the Materials Studio 2018 installation commands?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the commands have completed, you should log out of the cluster and log back in. Then you can load the module.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load the Materials Studio 2018 module?
qa_pairs.answer: You can load the Materials Studio 2018 module with the command `module load materialsstudio/2018`.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done to allow Materials Studio 2018 to access the licence server from compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: You will need to contact technical support so that they can configure the firewall(s) to allow the software to connect to your licence server.

qa_pairs.question: Who can install Materials Studio for a whole group?
qa_pairs.answer: A Principal Investigator (PI) who holds the Materials Studio licence can install it once for all their group members.

qa_pairs.question: Where is team work typically stored, and how does this relate to team installation of Materials Studio?
qa_pairs.answer: Team work is normally stored in the `/project` space, so you'll need to determine which project directory to use for the installation.

qa_pairs.question: How do I determine the actual path of a project directory named A_DIRECTORY for a team installation?
qa_pairs.answer: You can determine the actual path using the command `PI_PROJECT_DIR=$(readlink -f ~/projects/A_DIRECTORY)`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I determine the group of a project directory for a team installation?
qa_pairs.answer: You can determine the group using the command `PI_GROUP=$(stat -c%G $PI_PROJECT_DIR)`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step in installing Materials Studio for a team after determining the project directory and group values?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to change the default group to your team's `def-` group, for example, by running `newgrp $PI_GROUP`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I open the permissions of my project directory for team access during a team installation?
qa_pairs.answer: You can open the permissions of your project directory so your team can access it by running `chmod g+rsx $PI_PROJECT_DIR`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I create an installation directory within `/project` for a team installation of Materials Studio?
qa_pairs.answer: You can create an install directory within `/project` by running `mkdir $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018`.

qa_pairs.question: What command is used to install Materials Studio for a team?
qa_pairs.answer: The software can be installed using a command like `MS_LICENSE_SERVER=<port>@<server> eb MaterialsStudio-2018-dummy-dummy.eb --installpath=$PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018 --sourcepath=$HOME`.

qa_pairs.question: What command must be run before team members can use the team-installed Materials Studio software?
qa_pairs.answer: Before the software can be run, you must run the command `module use $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018/modules/2017/Core/`.

qa_pairs.question: How do team members load the Materials Studio module after it has been team-installed?
qa_pairs.answer: Team members can load the materialsstudio module by running `module load materialsstudio`.

qa_pairs.question: What should team members consider adding to their `~/.bashrc` file for easier access to team-installed Materials Studio?
qa_pairs.answer: Team members may wish to add the command `module use $PI_PROJECT_DIR/MatStudio2018/modules/2017/Core/` to their `~/.bashrc` file.

qa_pairs.question: What is an important note regarding the variables PI_GROUP and PI_PROJECT_DIR in a team installation?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to always replace the variables `PI_GROUP` and `PI_PROJECT_DIR` with their appropriate values.
